text: This is the current standard of care of abdominal wall closure.
Notice that the fascial layer is right above the internal organs.
Because using an exposed needle is dangerous, surgeons sometimes use this metal malleable, which serves as a cutting board to protect the organs.
Organs are on top of it, around it, just going everywhere.
Each time a stitch is placed, this malleable needs to be shifted around, which can actually cause trauma to the organs.
With this in mind, it's a challenge for surgeons to estimate even stitch placement, a one centimeter interval they like to follow.
